detailedExemplarData.title: How does the concentration of lemon juice affect its resistivity in an electrolyte solution?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data recording and processing are communicated clearly with labeled tables, units and uncertainties
Uncertainty propagation is handled correctly, with error bars and RÂ² values presented
Raw data averaging, CV validation and regression analysis demonstrate strong analytical understanding
weaknesses: Formatting issues (garbled symbols, inconsistent significant figures, missing unit labels) impede precision
One data point lacks an uncertainty estimate and unit conventions vary across tables
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates clear and mostly precise data communication, applies correct uncertainty propagation and processing, and justifies outlier treatment. Minor formatting inconsistencies and a missing uncertainty prevent a perfect mark, positioning the work solidly in band 5.

strengths: Research question is embedded in a specific, realâworld electrochemical context with clear motivation
Methodology is described in sufficient detail to allow reproducibility
Key methodological considerations (trials, concentration range, uncertainty propagation, outlier treatment, control variables) are fully explained
weaknesses: Minor ambiguities remain in probe measurement range and precise temperature control method
Occasional verbosity in background and hypothesis statements detracts from succinctness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although small omissions (exact conductivity range, detailed pH control) and a slightly wordy introduction prevent perfection, the student has clearly articulated a focused research question, provided a reproducible procedure and explained the methodological rationale. This meets all the requirements of the top band, meriting a score of 6 for Research Design.

strengths: Conclusion restates the quantitative inverse relationship coherently and links to the graphed trend and RÂ² value
Alignment of the conclusion with the hypothesis and data is clear
weaknesses: Comparison to accepted scientific context lacks depth (no literature values or discussion of underlying DebyeâHÃ¼ckel theory)
Numerical magnitude of slope/exponent is mentioned only briefly without detailed evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is justified and consistent with the analysis, fulfilling the criteria for band 4. However, the limited theoretical comparison and brevity on numerical detail mean it does not yet reach the higher band.

strengths: Identifies specific methodological weaknesses (temperature fluctuations, solution preparation, outliers)
Proposes realistic and relevant improvements (additional trials, broader concentration range, stricter controls)
weaknesses: Does not quantify the relative impact of each limitation on the results
Suggestions for improvement lack detail on implementation (e.g., exact number of additional trials)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation describes specific weaknesses and outlines realistic improvements, meeting the descriptors for band 4. The absence of quantitative impact assessment and detailed implementation prevents a higher mark.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student sets the research question clearly and embeds it in a focused, real-world context (electrolytic properties of citrus cells and potential battery applications).  Background notes link citric acid dissociation to conductivity and justify why resistivity is an interesting parameter, demonstrating specificity.  Minor digressions into historical anecdotes do not detract from the otherwise precise contextualisation, warranting the 5â6 band; slight verbosity keeps it at 5.

Absolute and percentage uncertainties are calculated for every mean, propagated correctly to resistivity and displayed as error bars.  Probe specifications and standard deviation justification are cited.  Minor gaps (missing uncertainty for one 60 % value) prevent a perfect 6, but consideration is largely appropriate.

Relevant methodological considerations are explained: three trials, ten concentrations spanning full range, justification of coefficient-of-variation threshold, propagation of uncertainties, identification of outliers, and control of temperature/volume/type.  Explanations show why each aspect is necessary to obtain sufficient data, although temperature control discussion is brief.  This fits the âexplainedâ descriptor, merit 5.

Specific weaknesses are described (temperature variation, residual solution, pulp impurities, outliers), but the explanation of their relative impact is minimalâstatements such as âcould have affected data, but not by muchâ lack quantitative assessment.  This aligns with âdescribes specific weaknessesâ (3â4 band) at the lower end.

A full materials list with quantities, equipment uncertainties, preparation table for every concentration and a step-by-step procedure (including cleaning protocol and repeats) would allow another student to reproduce the investigation.  Only minor ambiguities remain (exact probe range used, precise temperature control method), so the description meets the âallows reproductionâ criterion; given those small omissions, 5 rather than 6.

Raw data are averaged properly, CV used to validate means, reciprocal conversion to resistivity is correct, error propagation applied logically, and regressions with RÂ² are produced.  The outlier treatment is arguable yet justified in an appendix, so processing is mostly accurate, minor slips (unit label Î© m vs kÎ© m) cap score at 5.

Improvements are realistic (increase trials, widen concentration range, stricter control, double-check mobility calculations) and linked to identified issues, yet they are only briefly described without explaining how much each change would enhance reliability or validity.  Hence the strand meets âdescribedâ level, scoring 3.

Data tables include headings, units and uncertainties; processed data and graphs are labelled and legible.  However, typographical errors (e.g. garbled symbols, missing values) and inconsistent significant figures limit precision.  Thus communication is consistently clear but only intermittently precise, matching the 3â4 band, top of range.

The student compares results to theoretical expectations (ion concentration vs resistivity, anticipated mobility trend) and cites electrochemical theory, but depth is limitedâno literature values for citric acid conductivity or discussion of DebyeâHÃ¼ckel effects.  Comparison is relevant yet only partially developed, fitting the 3â4 band at 4.

The conclusion restates the quantitative inverse relationship and ties it directly to the graphed trend, RÂ² value and hypothesis.  It recognises the anomaly with ion-mobility findings and evaluates alignment.  The support is coherent and fully grounded in the analysis; slight brevity on numerical magnitude (slope, exponent) keeps it to 5.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly articulated and embedded in a focused electrochemical context, linking citric acid dissociation to resistivity in a lemonâbased battery application.
Safety considerations are thorough and clearly stated, covering chemical handling, ingestion avoidance, and spillage risks.
The definition of resistivity uses kÎ©Â·m but later units vary. State unit conventions consistently (for conductivities in S/m and resistivity Î©Â·m).
The raw data table shows Trial 3 at 0% concentration (161.806 ÂµS/cm) vastly lower than other trials, suggesting a possible measurement error or misâentry.
The use of standard deviation from the Vernier app as absolute uncertainty is reasonable, but include a brief rationale explaining why sensor scatter equates to measurement uncertainty.
Derivation of the coefficient of variation formula is accurate and cites a source for the 0â20% threshold, enhancing the rigour of uncertainty assessment.
The extensive historical introduction on Volta could be streamlined. Focus more directly on the electrochemical principles relevant to the investigation to maintain precision and context.

The ionisation equation is written as âHâ Cit â Hâº + Hâ Citâ but omits charge notation on citrate and the correct stoichiometry. It should read $$m H_3Citightarrow H^++H_2Cit^-$$ for clarity.
The hypothesis statement is wordy. Restate concisely: âIncreasing lemon juice concentration decreases electrolyte resistivity,â to maintain precision and readability.
Including pH control could strengthen reproducibility since citric acid dissociation and conductivity depend on acidity. Consider measuring and reporting pH for each solution.
The materials table omits the conductivity probeâs measurement range selection. Specify which range (low, mid, or high) was used to ensure reproducibility.
Unify the formatting of uncertainties in the equipment list: some are percentages, others are absolute. Consistent notation (e.g. Â±x unit) will improve clarity.
Step 5 in the procedure refers to repeating steps 2â3 but does not detail rinsing frequency or drying method. Clarify probe cleaning to avoid crossâcontamination.
The processed data table contains unrecognized symbols (e.g. overlines, L) that obscure uncertainties. Clean formatting to present values clearly.
Only the first three concentrations appear in the resistivity summary table. Present all data points to support the later trendâline regression.

The propagation of uncertainty for the reciprocal relationship is correctly derived as $$ rac{\\Delta\rho}{\rho}=\frac{\Delta\sigma}{\sigma}$$ and $$\\Delta \rho=\\rho\\frac{\\Delta\\sigma}{\\sigma}$$, demonstrating strong analytical understanding.
The resistivity table shows 10% uncertainty as â0.8â without unit; ensure each uncertainty carries consistent Î©Â·m notation and correct significant figures.
Add the equation of the trend line and the RÂ² value directly into Figure 2âs caption so the graph is selfâcontained and interpretable without reading the main text.
The estimate of citric acid concentration assumes a fixed 6.5% content. Provide justification for this choice and discuss its variability to strengthen the calculationâs validity.
The conclusion summarises resistivity results well but glosses over the unexpected mobility trend. Expand on theoretical reconciliation or propose hypotheses for this discrepancy.
The sources of error table omits mitigation strategies for âIncorrect preparation of solution.â Complete this cell to demonstrate how to reduce that error.
Strengths and weaknesses are identified, but quantitative impact of each limitation is not discussed. Explain which errors most affect validity and why.
Future improvements note wider ranges and stricter control. Specify target number of additional trials and concentration points to enhance statistical reliability.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
